sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: AIG commits up to $3.5bn to CVC partnership spanning credit and secondaries
feed.summary: American International Group (AIG) has formed a strategic partnership with CVC, committing up to $3.5 billion. This includes $2 billion for CVC's credit strategies and $1.5 billion as a cornerstone investment in CVC's new private equity secondaries evergreen platform. This collaboration marks AIG's first with a European-headquartered asset manager, providing the insurer with expanded access to alternative investments.

feed.title: Motorola Solutions expands AI-powered security platform
feed.summary: Motorola Solutions recently showcased its latest AI advancements at Intersec, demonstrating how its AI-powered security platform, featuring Avigilon Visual Alerts, helps critical enterprises move from reactive incident detection to proactive response. This technology allows security teams to create custom alerts for specific scenarios, bringing generative AI capabilities on-premise to enhance safety, compliance, and operational efficiency across various industries, including oil and gas and healthcare. The platform aims to transform video security into a site-specific operational engine, focusing on critical events and informed decision-making.

feed.title: Natural Gas Prices Spike Nearly 20% As Arctic Cold Slams US
feed.summary: U.S. natural gas prices surged by nearly 20% due to a sudden shift in late-January weather forecasts predicting much colder-than-normal conditions across the central and eastern United States. This arctic air is expected to significantly increase heating demand and electricity generation needs. Natural gas-linked stocks like EQT Corp., Chesapeake Energy Corp., Antero Resources Corp., and Range Resources Corp. are anticipated to react when markets reopen after the Martin Luther King Jr. Day holiday.

feed.title: KLA (KLAC) Is Up 9.8% After Analyst Upgrades On AI Chip Demand Narrative Shift?
feed.summary: KLA Corporation (KLAC) has seen its stock rise following analyst upgrades driven by strong demand for advanced AI chips and better-than-expected quarterly results. The upgrades highlight KLA's crucial role in the semiconductor equipment ecosystem, with its process control and inspection technology being essential for increasing chip complexity, particularly in AI and leading-edge foundries. While the increased focus on AI demand reshapes KLA's investment narrative, investors are also cautioned about the company's concentrated exposure to these advanced nodes and potential risks from customer capital expenditure slowdowns or geopolitical factors.

feed.title: Coca-Cola HBC Executives Boost Holdings Through Employee Share Purchase Plan
feed.summary: Coca-Cola HBC executives, including CEO Zoran Bogdanovic, acquired company shares through its Employee Share Purchase Plan (ESPP) on January 16, 2026, priced at £39.53418 per share. The company made matching contributions, reinforcing the alignment between management and shareholders. A recent analyst rating on GB:CCH stock is a "Buy" with a £4338.00 price target, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, also rates it as "Outperform" due to strong financials, positive technical indicators, and growth initiatives.

feed.title: AT&T class action alleges business customers billed for disconnected services
feed.summary: BHS Law LLP has filed a class action lawsuit against AT&T, alleging that the telecommunications giant improperly billed its business customers for phone services that were suspended, disconnected, or terminated. The lawsuit claims AT&T continued to charge customers while transitioning them from analog to digital networks, often without adequate notice. This alleged practice led to issues like lost fire-alarm monitoring and constitutes breach of contract and unfair business practices.

feed.title: NYSE parent unveils platform for round-the-clock tokenized securities trading
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), the parent company of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), has developed a new platform for trading and settling tokenized securities and is seeking regulatory approval for its launch. This platform aims to enable 24/7 trading of US-listed equities and ETFs with instant settlement, fractional shares, and stablecoin-based funding. The initiative is part of ICE's broader digital strategy to integrate blockchain technology into market infrastructure and support round-the-clock trading and collateral management.

feed.title: BullFrog AI and Myriad Uranium Interviews to Air on the RedChip Small Stocks, Big Money(TM) Show on Bloomberg TV
feed.summary: RedChip Companies will broadcast interviews with BullFrog AI, Inc. (Nasdaq:BFRG) and Myriad Uranium Corp. (OCTQB:MYRUF) on its "RedChip Small Stocks, Big Money™" show on Bloomberg TV this Saturday, November 29, at 7 p.m. ET. BullFrog AI's CEO will discuss their AI-driven drug development platform, highlighting recent milestones and growth potential in the biopharma R&D market. Myriad Uranium's CEO will outline the company's strategy for advancing its Copper Mountain and Red Basin uranium projects, emphasizing their significant resource estimates and market positioning amid increasing global demand for uranium.

feed.title: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights UnitedHealth Group, Centene, Humana and Elevance Health
feed.summary: This article from Zacks Investment Research discusses President Trump's "Great Healthcare Plan" and its potential impact on health insurers like UnitedHealth Group, Centene, Humana, and Elevance Health. The plan aims to reshape healthcare pricing, lower drug costs, reduce insurance premiums, and increase transparency, but faces criticism regarding its impact on individuals with pre-existing conditions and implementation details. Health insurers may experience pressure on profitability and increased compliance costs due to these proposed changes, with some currently holding "Sell" or "Strong Sell" Zacks Ranks.

feed.title: NYSE-parent Intercontinental Exchange develops platform for 24/7 tokenized securities trading
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (ICE), parent company of the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE), is developing a platform for round-the-clock trading of tokenized securities. The initiative aims to modernize financial markets by enabling continuous trading using blockchain technology. This move indicates a significant step by a major exchange operator into the digital asset space, potentially transforming traditional trading practices.

feed.title: Tripadvisor: Falling Behind In The Travel Race (Rating Downgrade) (NASDAQ:TRIP)
feed.summary: Tripadvisor's stock is downgraded to 'sell' due to its deteriorating fundamentals and loss of competitiveness in the online travel agency (OTA) market. The company's Q3 revenue growth missed expectations, with core segments turning negative, while competitors like Expedia and Booking Holdings are accelerating bookings. The author believes Tripadvisor's restructuring efforts are insufficient to reverse its underperformance.

feed.title: Berkshire Hathaway stock price: BRK.B faces Tuesday test after Trump tariff threat jolts markets
feed.summary: Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A, BRK.B) faces market uncertainty with the reopening of U.S. markets on Tuesday, following President Trump's new tariff threats on European goods. Despite Berkshire's reputation as a safe haven, its significant stock holdings could be impacted by global trade tensions. Investors are closely watching Tuesday's trading and upcoming inflation data for signals on interest rates and the potential effects of these tariffs.

feed.title: Expeditors International Earnings Preview: What to Expect
feed.summary: Expeditors International (EXPD) is set to release its fiscal 2025 fourth-quarter earnings by the end of February, with Wall Street anticipating a 13.1% year-over-year drop in Q4 EPS to $1.46. Despite this, the company has consistently beaten bottom-line estimates and has seen its shares skyrocket 46% over the past year, significantly outperforming the S&P 500 and the Industrial Select Sector SPDR Fund. Analysts currently have a consensus "Hold" rating on EXPD, even though its stock trades above the average price target, with some projecting a further 10.2% upside.

feed.title: Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Booking Holdings' Report
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) is scheduled to release its fiscal Q4 2025 earnings report soon, with analysts forecasting an adjusted EPS of $48.59, a 16.9% increase year-over-year. Despite the company consistently beating Wall Street's earnings estimates and reporting strong Q3 2025 results with a 14% rise in gross bookings, its stock has underperformed the S&P 500 and the Consumer Discretionary ETF over the past year. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating for BKNG, with an average price target suggesting a potential 20.8% upside.

feed.title: Previous Earnings Preview: What To Expect From Booking Holdings' Report
feed.summary: Booking Holdings Inc. (BKNG) is set to announce its fiscal Q4 2025 results, with analysts predicting an adjusted EPS of $48.59, marking a 16.9% increase year-over-year. The company has consistently beaten Wall Street's earnings estimates in the past four quarters. Despite outperforming in Q3 2025, the stock has underperformed the S&P 500 Index and the State Street Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR ETF over the last 52 weeks, yet analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with a target suggesting a 20.8% potential upside.

feed.title: Permian Resources Up 17% in 3 Months: Should You Buy or Hold?
feed.summary: Permian Resources (PR) has seen a 16.8% increase in its share price over the past three months, outperforming the broader oil and energy sector. The company's strong performance is attributed to its low-cost structure, operational efficiency, robust free cash flow, strong balance sheet, and disciplined acquisition strategy. However, investors are cautioned about potential risks such as industry-wide cost inflation, acquisition integration challenges, uncertainties in reserve estimates, and exposure to commodity price volatility, suggesting a "Hold" rating.

feed.title: Should Investors Be Drawn to Cheap Valuation of McDonald's Stock?
feed.summary: McDonald's stock is currently trading at a valuation discount compared to the restaurant industry average, with a forward 12-month P/E ratio of 23.02 versus 24.64. While the stock has outperformed the broader restaurant industry in the last six months, it has lagged the S&P 500. Investors are weighing whether this lower valuation presents an opportunity to acquire a global leader or reflects short-term challenges due to macro pressures and margin concerns.

feed.title: F5 tackles AI security with new platform extensions
feed.summary: F5 is expanding its Application Delivery and Security Platform with new products focusing on AI security and multi-cloud operations. The company introduced F5 AI Guardrails and F5 AI Red Team, acquired through CalypsoAI, to protect AI systems from unique threats like prompt injection and jailbreaking. Additionally, F5 NGINXaaS, a web-server-as-a-service offering, is expanding its availability to Google Cloud and plans to support AWS, addressing customer demand for consistent tooling across cloud providers.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Says You Should Buy the Dip in Broadcom Stock
feed.summary: Wells Fargo analysts have upgraded Broadcom (AVGO) to "Overweight" from "Equal-weight" and increased their price target due to growing confidence in the company's execution and increasing visibility into future catalysts. This decision follows a recent 4.2% dip in Broadcom's stock, which Wells Fargo views as an attractive entry point for investors. The positive outlook is primarily driven by accelerating AI momentum, with Wells Fargo significantly raising its AI semiconductor revenue projections for 2026 and 2027, anticipating substantial year-over-year growth.

feed.title: UnitedHealth Stock Has Fallen 36%. Why Bernstein Calls It a Top Pick for 2026
feed.summary: Bernstein analyst Lance Wilkes has named UnitedHealth (UNH) his top healthcare pick for 2026, despite the stock falling 36% over the past year. Wilkes maintains an Outperform rating and a $444 price target, anticipating a recovery in Medicare Advantage and Medicaid margins and improvement from exiting underperforming operations. He also noted the company's flexibility to adjust its structure and the long-term growth potential of Optum, suggesting a potential 33% upside.

feed.title: Microchip Expands PolarFire® FPGA Smart Embedded Video Ecosystem with New SDI IP Cores and Quad CoaXPress™ Bridge Kit
feed.summary: Microchip has expanded its PolarFire FPGA smart embedded video ecosystem by introducing new SDI IP cores and a quad CoaXPress bridge kit. These additions provide reliable, low-power, and high-bandwidth video connectivity for demanding applications like medical diagnostics, industrial vision, and robotics. The solution stacks aim to streamline development, enhance security, and accelerate time to market for OEMs creating compact, fanless, and high-performance video systems.

feed.title: Gartner: Global AI spending to reach $2.5 trillion in 2026
feed.summary: Gartner projects global AI spending to hit $2.52 trillion in 2026, marking a 44% increase from the previous year, largely driven by investments in AI infrastructure, particularly AI-optimized servers. The report suggests that AI is currently in the "trough of disillusionment" and its adoption will depend on proven business value and integration through existing software vendors rather than standalone ventures. Significant scaling in larger organizations will occur when ROI becomes more predictable.

feed.title: What to Expect From Consolidated Edison's Q4 2025 Earnings Report
feed.summary: Consolidated Edison, Inc. (ED) is set to announce its Q4 2025 earnings on February 19, with analysts expecting a profit of $0.84 per share, a 14.3% decrease year-over-year. Despite lagging the S&P 500, ED has outperformed the Utilities Select Sector SPDR ETF and has a "Hold" rating from analysts with a mean price target of $104.41. The company previously beat Q3 expectations and raised its fiscal 2025 EPS guidance.

feed.title: Costco Confirms Stores Will Remain Open on MLK Day
feed.summary: Costco Wholesale has announced that its U.S. warehouses, including petrol stations and pharmacies, will operate under normal business hours on Martin Luther King Jr. Day 2026, distinguishing itself from federal services and financial institutions that will close. This decision provides a strategic advantage for Costco, capitalizing on increased foot traffic from those who have the day off from other sectors. Shoppers are advised to check local store hours as they may vary.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $80,000 of CISCO SYSTEMS INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: A recent Lobbying Disclosure Act filing revealed that CISCO SYSTEMS INC. disclosed $80,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, primarily on issues affecting technology companies. The article also details congressional stock trading activity in CSCO, notable insider trading sales by executives, and significant hedge fund movements in their CSCO positions. Analyst ratings and price targets for CSCO are also provided, with a median target of $90.0 from 13 analysts.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $30,000 of TIBER CREEK GROUP ON BEHALF OF MERCK & CO. INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: A recent lobbying disclosure revealed that TIBER CREEK GROUP lobbied for Merck & Co. Inc. (MRK) in Q4 2025, spending $30,000 on issues concerning drug pricing. The article also details recent congressional and insider trading activity involving MRK stock, as well as hedge fund movements and analyst ratings and price targets for the company.

feed.title: How ExxonMobil Keeps Rewarding Shareholders Across Various Oil Cycles
feed.summary: Exxon Mobil Corporation consistently rewards shareholders despite its upstream business's vulnerability to commodity price volatility. The company achieves this through 43 consecutive years of dividend increases—making it the second-largest dividend payer among S&P 500 companies—and aggressive share repurchases, with plans for $20 billion in buybacks in 2025 and 2026. This resilience is attributed to its access to low-cost oil and gas resources and a strong balance sheet.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $30,000 of TIBER CREEK GROUP ON BEHALF OF GILEAD SCIENCES INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: Tiber Creek Group disclosed $30,000 in lobbying on behalf of Gilead Sciences Inc. for Q4 2025, focusing on HIV medications and drug pricing. The article also provides recent congressional stock trading, insider trading activity, hedge fund movements, and analyst ratings/price targets for GILD stock. This update highlights various financial and political activities related to Gilead Sciences.

feed.title: Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc.: BCC Research Releases Comprehensive 360° Company Analysis
feed.summary: BCC Research has released a comprehensive 360° Company Analysis Report titled “A Deep-Dive Strategic Profile of Thermo Fisher Scientific Inc.: Scientific Leadership, Global Scale, and Transformative Innovation.” This report extensively details Thermo Fisher’s business model, financial performance, global reach, product ecosystem, R&D direction, sustainability commitments, and long-term strategic priorities across its four major segments: Life Sciences Solutions, Analytical Instruments, Specialty Diagnostics, and Laboratory Products & Biopharma Services. The analysis also covers financial outlook, M&A activity, ESG framework, and a SWOT analysis, positioning Thermo Fisher as a leader in scientific discovery, clinical care, and advanced manufacturing.

feed.title: Lobbying Update: $740,000 of PALO ALTO NETWORKS INC. lobbying was just disclosed
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks Inc. (PANW) disclosed $740,000 in lobbying expenditures for Q4 2025, focusing on cybersecurity threats, funding for cyber resilience in federal defense and homeland security, and AI policy. The article also provides recent congressional stock trading activity in PANW, insider trading details showing significant sales, and hedge fund movements in the stock. Analyst ratings and price targets for PANW are also summarized, with a median target of $232.0.

feed.title: Adirondack chairs sold at Lowe’s, Home Depot recalled over fall risk
feed.summary: Thousands of Adirondack patio chairs sold at Lowe's and Home Depot are being recalled due to a fall and injury risk. The U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission stated that the chairs, sold under the brand names Adams RealComfort and StyleWell, can crack and collapse. Consumers who purchased these chairs between August and October 2025 are advised to immediately stop using them and return them for a full refund.

feed.title: Syngene expands research collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb
feed.summary: Syngene International has extended its research collaboration with Bristol Myers Squibb until 2035, broadening the scope of integrated services across the drug development lifecycle. This expansion solidifies Syngene's role as a strategic partner, delivering end-to-end scientific and manufacturing solutions. The partnership, which began in 1998, aims to accelerate the delivery of new medicines globally.

feed.title: Kraft Heinz Fries Box With Sauce Pocket: Genius or Gimmick?
feed.summary: Kraft Heinz has launched the "Heinz Dipper," a French fries box with a built-in sauce compartment designed for on-the-go dipping without messes. This innovation addresses the common problem of spilling condiments, with 70% of consumers admitting to spills. The Dipper leverages the iconic Heinz Keystone shape and will debut in 11 countries, receiving overwhelmingly positive reactions from consumers and design experts as a practical and intuitive solution.

feed.title: Waste Management Market to Grow at 6% CAGR Through 2031 Amid Sustainability Push, Says Mordor Intelligence
feed.summary: The waste management market is projected to grow from USD 1.52 trillion in 2026 to USD 2.09 trillion by 2031, with a CAGR of 6.58%, driven by sustainability efforts, regulatory policies, and a shift towards resource recovery. Asia-Pacific currently holds the largest market share, while the Middle East and Africa are emerging as the fastest-growing regions. Key drivers include extended producer responsibility in APAC and increasing demand for waste transparency influencing corporate strategies and competitive positioning among service providers.

feed.title: Micron spends $1.8 billion on PSMC Fab to boost DRAM production
feed.summary: Micron has signed an exclusive Letter of Intent to acquire Powerchip Semiconductor Manufacturing Company's (PSMC) P5 fabrication site in Taiwan for $1.8 billion. This acquisition will provide Micron with existing 300mm fab cleanroom space, allowing them to accelerate their DRAM production expansion plans and address global demand. The transaction is expected to close by Q2 2026, with meaningful DRAM output from the new site anticipated in the second half of 2027.

feed.title: Coca-Cola's Premiumization Push: Growth Engine or Volume Risk?
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is increasingly adopting a premiumization strategy to drive revenue growth, expand margins, and counteract slow sales volumes. The company is focusing on offering a diverse range of products, from affordable local options to premium and functional beverages, to meet changing consumer preferences. This approach, along with innovation and marketing, aims to maintain Coca-Cola's market leadership despite current soft volumes across key markets, though the strategy faces competition from PepsiCo and Keurig Dr Pepper who are also pursuing premiumization.

feed.title: The Nuclear Renaissance: Why Vistra Corp is the Bedrock of the AI Era
feed.summary: Vistra Corp (NYSE: VST) has transformed from a struggling utility to a key player in the AI revolution, driven by its strategic focus on nuclear and gas-fired power, particularly after its acquisition of Energy Harbor. The company's integrated retail and generation model, coupled with significant cash flow and a landmark nuclear agreement with Meta Platforms, positions it as an essential infrastructure provider for the digital economy. Vistra's stock performance has been exceptional, reflecting its status as a "Utility 2.0" champion that offers investors defensive utility characteristics alongside AI-driven growth opportunities.

feed.title: Zacks.com featured highlights include Nu Holdings, Amazon.com and Microsoft
feed.summary: This article highlights Nu Holdings (NU), Amazon.com (AMZN), and Microsoft (MSFT) as strong momentum stocks to buy, based on Richard Driehaus's "buy high and sell higher" investment philosophy. These companies have a Zacks Rank #2 and a Momentum Score of B, indicating strong uptrends and impressive earnings performance. The Driehaus strategy emphasizes positive 50-day moving averages, strong earnings growth, and a history of beating estimates for identifying potential outperformers.

feed.title: AI, Digital Tools and Hub Data: Could ISRG Evolve Beyond Robotics?
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical's new da Vinci 5 platform integrates advanced AI and digital tools with significantly more compute power to collect and process vast amounts of surgical data. This strategic move suggests the company is looking to evolve beyond its robotics-focused origins into a surgical data intelligence firm, using AI to provide real-time intraoperative guidance. Other medical device companies like GE HealthCare and Boston Scientific are also actively incorporating AI to enhance their product offerings and improve efficiency.

feed.title: Caterpillar announces group president of Construction Industries transition
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. announced that Tony Fassino, group president of Construction Industries, will retire effective May 31, 2026. Rod Shurman, currently senior vice president of Caterpillar Building Construction Products, will succeed Fassino as group president of Construction Industries, effective February 1, 2026. Fassino had a 30-year career with Caterpillar, holding various positions, while Shurman has been with the company since 1997 and held several leadership roles in engineering and operations.

feed.title: P/E Ratio Insights for Stryker
feed.summary: Stryker (NYSE: SYK) shares are currently trading at $363.42, a 0.26% increase in the current session. The article discusses the use of the P/E ratio to assess a company's market performance, noting that Stryker's P/E is lower than its industry's aggregate. It concludes that while a low P/E can indicate undervaluation, it also has limitations and should be used with other financial metrics for investment decisions.

feed.title: Assessing Intel's Performance Against Competitors In Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment Industry
feed.summary: This article analyzes Intel's performance against its competitors in the Semiconductors & Semiconductor Equipment industry, examining key financial metrics like P/E, P/B, P/S ratios, ROE, EBITDA, gross profit, and revenue growth. While Intel shows a high P/E ratio suggesting overvaluation and strong EBITDA, its lower ROE, gross profit, and significantly lower revenue growth compared to the industry average indicate potential challenges in profitability and sales expansion. The analysis also highlights Intel's stronger financial position regarding its debt-to-equity ratio compared to its top peers.

feed.title: LVMH slides in Paris trading after Morgan Stanley pulls its bull rating (LVMUY:OTCMKTS)
feed.summary: LVMH Moët Hennessy - Louis Vuitton (LVMHF, LVMUY) saw its stock slide in Paris trading after Morgan Stanley downgraded it to an Equal-weight rating from Overweight. Analyst Edouard Aubin cited greater downside risk to EPS consensus numbers for the change.

feed.title: Gartner: Global AI spending to reach $2.5 trillion in 2026
feed.summary: Gartner projects global AI spending to hit $2.52 trillion in 2026, marking a 44% increase from the previous year. This growth is significantly driven by investments in AI infrastructure, particularly AI-optimized servers. Gartner notes that AI is currently in the "trough of disillusionment" and expects future investments to be based on proven business value rather than speculative pilot projects.

feed.title: Comfort Systems USA Reports Record $9.38 Billion Backlog, 20% Dividend Increase
feed.summary: Comfort Systems USA has reported a record $9.38 billion backlog, marking a 65% year-over-year increase, driven by strong demand in HVAC and electrical services, particularly for AI data centers. The company also announced a 20% dividend increase for 2025, reflecting solid cash flow and a commitment to shareholder returns. Analyst forecasts indicate a positive outlook for the stock, with an average 1-year price target of $1163 USD.

feed.title: Analysts’ Opinions Are Mixed on These Communication Services Stocks: Cogeco Communications (OtherCGEAF) and Live Nation Entertainment (LYV)
feed.summary: This article examines analyst ratings for two communication services stocks: Cogeco Communications (CGEAF) and Live Nation Entertainment (LYV). Cogeco Communications received a "Hold" consensus with a modest upside, while Live Nation Entertainment garnered a "Strong Buy" consensus with a significant upside, according to recent analyst reports. The article details specific analyst recommendations, price targets, and their success rates for each company.

feed.title: What Makes Amazon (AMZN) an Attractive Long-Term Investment Opportunity?
feed.summary: Wedgewood Partners, in its Q4 2025 investor letter, highlighted Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) as an attractive long-term investment, noting its improved cash flow returns, disciplined management, and potential for double-digit growth in e-commerce and IaaS. The firm believes Amazon's current valuation multiples are below historical levels despite strong margin expansion, driven by both advertising and cost management. While acknowledging Amazon's appeal, Wedgewood Partners also indicated that some AI stocks might offer higher returns in a shorter timeframe.

feed.title: NYSE targets weekend investors with new blockchain platform for 24/7 stock trading
feed.summary: The NYSE is planning to launch a new blockchain-based platform this year to enable 24/7 stock trading. This initiative aims to cater to weekend investors and expand trading hours beyond the traditional weekday schedule.

feed.title: Shareholders approve all proposals at Coca-Cola HBC extraordinary meeting
feed.summary: Coca-Cola HBC AG shareholders overwhelmingly approved all three proposals at an extraordinary general meeting. The approved measures include a capital band provision, allowing the use and transfer of treasury shares, and amendments to the Articles of Association for governance changes. These actions facilitate a Sale and Purchase Agreement and an Option Agreement, with 71.18% of voting rights represented.

feed.title: Earnings week ahead: Netflix takes the spotlight, but these reports are also on watch (NFLX:NASDAQ)
feed.summary: The upcoming week features a busy earnings season with Netflix (NFLX) in the spotlight, alongside reports from tech, healthcare, energy, and industrial sectors. Investors will be keenly watching subscriber growth and spending for Netflix, while other companies like Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) face valuation concerns despite strong stock performance. Guidance from GE Aerospace (GE) on delivery targets will be crucial for its long-term valuation assessment.
